title: Bridge Essay: The microbiota-gut-brain axis in sleep disorders.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Liberation/Enlightenment** (Phase 15) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Liberation/Enlightenment to mechanistic neuroscience.

**The microbiota-gut-brain axis in sleep disorders.** — Sleep Med Rev. PMID: 36099873. DOI: 10.1016/j.smrv.2022.101691. Sleep is acomplex physiological process and is acritical determinant of physical and mental health. In the past decades, significant progress has been made in understanding the neural mechanisms of sleep and awakening. However, the initiation and maintenance of the sleep-wake cycle is regulated not only by the central system but is also affected by signals from peripheral tissues.
